reason: Both correctly state exactly-once delivery is impossible, distinguish delivery vs. processing, and prescribe the standard at-least-once + idempotent consumer approach with dedup/outbox-inbox patterns and caveats about Kafka/Flink-style "exactly-once" being scoped to their own ecosystems. No factual errors in either. Answer A is slightly more structured with an explicit sketch and names the inbox/outbox pattern directly; Answer B is a touch more concise and adds a useful note about external side effects (email/HTTP) needing separate idempotency handling. The two are comparably strong with only minor stylistic differences, so this is a genuine tie.
